tool_input.command:
cd /Users/Johnsonpampers/samus/projects/vnzla-oil && git add app/dashboard.tsx && git commit -m "Fix: load Chart.js and Leaflet via DOM script injection instead of next/Script

The lazyOnload strategy wasn't executing the dashboard JS reliably.
Now loads libs sequentially via createElement, then injects dashboard
JS as inline script after both Chart.js and Leaflet are on window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"

All Producers
CompanyOriginOFACProd (bpd)2026 GuidanceShareKey JVs
Export Destinations (2025 Avg)
Output by Country of Origin + Upside
Empresas Mixtas — JV Output (K bpd)
Infrastructure Utilization
Paraguana Refinery (955K design)~20%
Petropiar Upgrader~65%
Petrocedeño Upgrader (key upside)~25%
Storage Tanks~66%
Pipeline Network~40%
Technical Workforce (vs 2014)~30%
$10–20B and several years needed before output exceeds 1.5M bpd. Only 2/4 Orinoco upgraders partially functional. GL-47 authorizes diluent exports. — CFR, Rystad
Financial Overview
ExxonMobil CEO: "uninvestable." ConocoPhillips owed ~$10B. No firm capex commitments from Big Six. Trump's $100B investment claim unverified. — Fortune, CNBC
Critical Context
POST-MADUROCaptured Jan 3 by US forces. Rodríguez (SDN-listed) acting president. New hydrocarbon law ends PDVSA monopoly. Independent arbitration allowed.
REVENUE CONTROLEO 14373: All oil revenue settles in US-controlled Treasury accounts. $1B collected. $5B projected. $150B potential over 10yr.
HORMUZ CRISISStrait disrupted since Mar 2. Brent +60% MTD — largest monthly surge since '88. Venezuela supply suddenly strategic to US.
OPEC STATUSExempt from quotas since 2019. Could produce 1.5M+ bpd freely. Reserves: 303B bbl (#1). Constraint is capital, not geology.
Infrastructure Map
Click to interact with map
Oil Upgrader Gas Refinery Terminal
Sanctions & Policy Timeline
MAR 5, 2026
Shell signs oil/gas cooperation deals with PDVSA — witnessed by US Interior Sec. Burgum
FEB 18
GL-50A: Maurel & Prom added to Big Six (Pertamina-owned, initially excluded)
FEB 13
GL-49/50: Big Six authorized for full Venezuela oil/gas ops. GL-47 authorizes diluent exports
FEB 12
Trump disavows Harry Sargeant III (NABEP) on Truth Social
JAN 9
EO 14373: All Venezuelan oil revenue to US-controlled Treasury accounts
JAN 3
Maduro captured by US special forces. Rodríguez assumes presidency
NOV 2025
Assembly approves 15-yr Russian JV extension through 2041
JUL 2025
OFAC issues new license — Chevron JVs resume ops
APR 2025
25% US tariff on Venezuelan oil importers
MAR 2025
OFAC revokes Repsol, ENI, M&P licenses
FEB 2025
Trump revokes Chevron Biden-era GL-41
GL-50A — "Big Six"
BPUK · New entrant
ChevronUS · 242K bpd
ENIIT · Resuming
RepsolES · Resuming
ShellNL · Dragon Gas
Maurel & PromFR · Feb 18
US-law governed contracts only. Payments to OFAC Foreign Government Deposit Funds. No Chinese/Russian/Iranian counterparties. 10-day + 90-day reporting requirements.
Active Deals (Q1 2026)
Shell — Dragon Gas: 350 mmcfd offshore to Trinidad LNG. FID possible 2026. First gas 2027–28.
Shell — Carito/Pirital: 94K bpd crude + 1 Bcfd gas. 350 mmcfd currently flared.
Chevron — Ayacucho 8: Orinoco expansion. Reduced royalties. Review end-Mar.
CCRC — Maracaibo: $1B, 500 wells, 60K target end-2026. Status uncertain post-Jan.
Debt Exposure
Bondholders (prin+int)~$100B
China (outstanding)$10–15B
Russia (outstanding)~$5B
ConocoPhillips (arb.)~$10B
Total external~$160B
Data: EIA, OPEC, OFAC, CEIC, Trading Economics, Kpler, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Rystad, S&P Global, Mayer Brown, Cleary Gottlieb, Crowell & Moring  |  Updated 31 Mar 2026  |  View All Sources →
